sternocleidomastoid.action: Unilateral: lateral flexion, contralateral rotation. Bilateral: neck flexion

platysma.origin: Fascia of deltoid and pectoralis major
platysma.action: Depresses mandible and corners of mouth; tenses skin of neck

digastric.origin: Anterior belly: digastric fossa of mandible. Posterior belly: mastoid process
digastric.innervation: Anterior: mylohyoid nerve (CN V3). Posterior: facial nerve (CN VII)

mylohyoid.action: Elevates hyoid, floor of mouth; depresses mandible

sternohyoid.origin: Manubrium of sternum and medial clavicle

thyrohyoid.insertion: Body and greater horn of hyoid bone

splenius capitis.insertion: Mastoid process, lateral superior nuchal line
splenius capitis.action: Unilateral: lateral flexion, ipsilateral rotation. Bilateral: extension of head
splenius capitis.innervation: Posterior rami of middle cervical nerves

splenius cervicis.action: Extends and rotates the cervical spine
splenius cervicis.innervation: Posterior rami of lower cervical nerves

levator veli palatini.origin: Petrous part of temporal bone, medial auditory tube
levator veli palatini.action: Elevates soft palate during swallowing and speech
levator veli palatini.innervation: Vagus nerve (CN X) via pharyngeal plexus

tensor veli palatini.origin: Scaphoid fossa, medial auditory tube cartilage
tensor veli palatini.action: Tenses soft palate, opens auditory tube

scalenus anterior.origin: Anterior tubercles of transverse processes C3-C6
scalenus anterior.action: Elevates first rib; lateral flexion and rotation of neck
scalenus anterior.innervation: Anterior rami of cervical nerves C5-C7

scalenus medius.origin: Posterior tubercles of transverse processes C2-C7
scalenus medius.insertion: Superior surface of first rib (behind subclavian artery groove)
scalenus medius.action: Elevates first rib; lateral flexion of neck
scalenus medius.innervation: Anterior rami of cervical nerves C3-C8

scalenus posterior.origin: Posterior tubercles of transverse processes C4-C6
scalenus posterior.action: Elevates second rib; lateral flexion of neck
scalenus posterior.innervation: Anterior rami of cervical nerves C6-C8

geniohyoid.origin: Inferior mental spine (genial tubercle) of mandible
geniohyoid.action: Elevates and protracts hyoid; depresses mandible when hyoid is fixed

sternothyroid.origin: Posterior surface of manubrium of sternum
sternothyroid.action: Depresses larynx after swallowing or vocalization

stylohyoid.insertion: Body of hyoid bone (at junction with greater horn)
stylohyoid.action: Elevates and retracts hyoid bone during swallowing

longus capitis.origin: Anterior tubercles of transverse processes C3-C6
longus capitis.innervation: Anterior rami of cervical nerves C1-C3

longus colli.origin: Anterior vertebral bodies and transverse processes C1-T3
longus colli.insertion: Anterior tubercle of atlas (C1), vertebral bodies C2-C4, transverse processes C5-C6
longus colli.action: Flexion of cervical spine; slight rotation and lateral flexion
longus colli.innervation: Anterior rami of cervical nerves C2-C6

lateral crico-arytenoid.action: Adducts vocal cords; closes rima glottidis

posterior crico-arytenoid.origin: Posterior surface of cricoid cartilage lamina
posterior crico-arytenoid.action: Abducts vocal cords; only opener of rima glottidis

thyro-arytenoid.action: Relaxes and shortens vocal cords; adducts vocal folds

transverse arytenoid.origin: Posterior surface of one arytenoid cartilage
transverse arytenoid.insertion: Posterior surface of opposite arytenoid cartilage
transverse arytenoid.action: Adducts arytenoid cartilages; closes posterior rima glottidis

oblique arytenoid.origin: Muscular process of one arytenoid cartilage
oblique arytenoid.action: Adducts arytenoid cartilages; closes laryngeal inlet

cricothyroid.origin: Anterior and lateral surfaces of cricoid arch
cricothyroid.insertion: Inferior horn and lower lamina of thyroid cartilage
cricothyroid.action: Tenses and elongates vocal cords; raises pitch

frontalis.insertion: Skin of eyebrows and root of nose
frontalis.action: Raises eyebrows; wrinkles forehead skin (expression of surprise)
frontalis.innervation: Temporal branch of facial nerve (CN VII)

orbicularis oculi.origin: Orbital: medial orbital margin, medial palpebral ligament. Palpebral: medial palpebral ligament, lacrimal bone
orbicularis oculi.insertion: Circular (returns to origin); lateral palpebral raphe
orbicularis oculi.action: Orbital part: forceful eye closure. Palpebral part: gentle blinking
orbicularis oculi.innervation: Temporal and zygomatic branches of facial nerve (CN VII)

orbicularis oris.origin: Maxilla and mandible; blends with other perioral muscles
orbicularis oris.insertion: Skin and mucous membrane of lips
orbicularis oris.action: Closes and protrudes lips; essential for speech, eating, and kissing
orbicularis oris.innervation: Buccal and marginal mandibular branches of facial nerve (CN VII)

zygomaticus major.action: Draws angle of mouth superiorly and laterally (smiling)
zygomaticus major.innervation: Buccal branch of facial nerve (CN VII)

zygomaticus minor.origin: Lateral surface of zygomatic bone (anterior to zygomaticus major)
zygomaticus minor.insertion: Upper lip, lateral to levator labii superioris
zygomaticus minor.action: Elevates upper lip; deepens nasolabial furrow
zygomaticus minor.innervation: Buccal branch of facial nerve (CN VII)

levator labii superioris.insertion: Skin and muscle of upper lip
levator labii superioris.innervation: Buccal branch of facial nerve (CN VII)

depressor labii inferioris.origin: Oblique line of mandible, between mental foramen and symphysis
depressor labii inferioris.insertion: Skin and mucosa of lower lip
depressor labii inferioris.innervation: Marginal mandibular branch of facial nerve (CN VII)

depressor anguli oris.origin: Oblique line of mandible (below mental foramen)
depressor anguli oris.innervation: Marginal mandibular branch of facial nerve (CN VII)

risorius.origin: Parotid fascia and skin over masseter
risorius.action: Retracts angle of mouth; produces a false smile or grimace
risorius.innervation: Buccal branch of facial nerve (CN VII)

mentalis.action: Protrudes lower lip; wrinkles chin skin (pouting)
mentalis.innervation: Marginal mandibular branch of facial nerve (CN VII)

procerus.origin: Nasal bone and upper lateral nasal cartilage
procerus.action: Depresses medial eyebrow; produces transverse wrinkles over bridge of nose
procerus.innervation: Temporal branch of facial nerve (CN VII)

nasalis.origin: Transverse part: maxilla lateral to nasal notch. Alar part: maxilla over lateral incisor
nasalis.insertion: Transverse part: aponeurosis on bridge of nose. Alar part: alar cartilage
nasalis.action: Transverse part: compresses nostril. Alar part: dilates nostril (flaring)
nasalis.innervation: Buccal branch of facial nerve (CN VII)

corrugator supercilii.origin: Medial end of superciliary arch of frontal bone
corrugator supercilii.innervation: Temporal branch of facial nerve (CN VII)

temporalis.origin: Temporal fossa and deep temporal fascia
temporalis.insertion: Coronoid process and anterior ramus of mandible
temporalis.action: Elevation and retraction of mandible; unilateral: ipsilateral deviation
temporalis.innervation: Deep temporal nerves from mandibular nerve (CN V3)

masseter.origin: Superficial part: zygomatic arch (anterior two-thirds). Deep part: zygomatic arch (posterior third)
masseter.insertion: Lateral surface of ramus and angle of mandible
masseter.action: Elevation and protrusion of mandible; powerful jaw closing for mastication
masseter.innervation: Masseteric nerve from mandibular nerve (CN V3)

medial pterygoid.origin: Deep head: medial surface of lateral pterygoid plate. Superficial head: maxillary tuberosity
medial pterygoid.insertion: Medial surface of ramus and angle of mandible
medial pterygoid.action: Elevation of mandible; assists in protrusion and lateral movements
medial pterygoid.innervation: Medial pterygoid nerve from mandibular nerve (CN V3)

lateral pterygoid.origin: Superior head: infratemporal surface of sphenoid. Inferior head: lateral surface of lateral pterygoid plate
lateral pterygoid.insertion: Superior head: articular disc and capsule of TMJ. Inferior head: pterygoid fovea of mandible
lateral pterygoid.action: Protrusion and depression of mandible; lateral movements for grinding
lateral pterygoid.innervation: Lateral pterygoid nerve from mandibular nerve (CN V3)

deltoid.origin: Lateral clavicle, acromion, spine of scapula
deltoid.action: Anterior: flexion, medial rotation. Middle: abduction. Posterior: extension, lateral rotation

supraspinatus.insertion: Superior facet of greater tubercle of humerus

infraspinatus.insertion: Middle facet of greater tubercle of humerus
infraspinatus.action: Lateral rotation of shoulder; stabilizes humeral head

subscapularis.action: Medial rotation of arm; stabilizes shoulder joint
subscapularis.innervation: Upper and lower subscapular nerves (C5, C6)

teres major.origin: Inferior angle and lower lateral border of scapula
teres major.insertion: Medial lip of intertubercular groove of humerus
teres major.action: Adduction, extension, and medial rotation of arm

teres minor.origin: Middle part of lateral border of scapula
teres minor.insertion: Inferior facet of greater tubercle of humerus
teres minor.action: Lateral rotation of arm; stabilizes shoulder

levator scapulae.action: Elevates scapula; tilts glenoid cavity inferiorly

subclavius.origin: First rib at junction with its costal cartilage
subclavius.insertion: Inferior surface of middle third of clavicle
subclavius.action: Depresses and stabilizes clavicle; protects underlying neurovascular structures

pectoralis major.origin: Clavicle, sternum, costal cartilages 1-6, external oblique aponeurosis
pectoralis major.insertion: Lateral lip of intertubercular groove of humerus
pectoralis major.action: Flexion, adduction, and medial rotation of arm

pectoralis minor.action: Depresses, protracts, and downwardly rotates scapula

serratus anterior.insertion: Costal surface of medial border of scapula
serratus anterior.action: Protracts and upwardly rotates scapula; stabilizes scapula to thorax

trapezius.origin: External occipital protuberance, nuchal ligament, spinous processes C7-T12
trapezius.insertion: Lateral clavicle, acromion, spine of scapula
trapezius.action: Upper: elevates scapula. Middle: retracts scapula. Lower: depresses scapula

rhomboid major.action: Retracts, elevates, and downwardly rotates scapula

rhomboid minor.origin: Spinous processes of C7-T1, nuchal ligament
rhomboid minor.insertion: Medial border of scapula (at spine level)

serratus posterior superior.origin: Spinous processes of C7-T3, nuchal ligament

latissimus dorsi.origin: Spinous processes T7-L5, thoracolumbar fascia, iliac crest, inferior angle of scapula, ribs 9-12
latissimus dorsi.insertion: Floor of intertubercular groove of humerus
latissimus dorsi.action: Extension, adduction, and medial rotation of arm; assists in respiration

erector spinae.origin: Sacrum, iliac crest, lumbar and lower thoracic spinous processes
erector spinae.insertion: Ribs, cervical and thoracic transverse/spinous processes, mastoid process
erector spinae.action: Bilateral: extension of vertebral column. Unilateral: lateral flexion

iliocostalis cervicis.action: Extension and lateral flexion of cervical spine
iliocostalis cervicis.innervation: Posterior rami of cervical and upper thoracic spinal nerves

iliocostalis thoracis.insertion: Angles of ribs 1-6 and transverse process of C7
iliocostalis thoracis.action: Extension and lateral flexion of thoracic spine; stabilizes ribs
iliocostalis thoracis.innervation: Posterior rami of thoracic spinal nerves

iliocostalis lumborum.insertion: Angles of ribs 6-12 and transverse processes of L1-L4
iliocostalis lumborum.action: Extension and lateral flexion of lumbar spine
iliocostalis lumborum.innervation: Posterior rami of lumbar and lower thoracic spinal nerves

longissimus capitis.origin: Transverse processes of T1-T5, articular processes of C4-C7
longissimus capitis.action: Extension and lateral flexion of head; ipsilateral rotation
longissimus capitis.innervation: Posterior rami of middle and lower cervical spinal nerves

longissimus cervicis.action: Extension and lateral flexion of cervical spine
longissimus cervicis.innervation: Posterior rami of cervical and upper thoracic spinal nerves

longissimus thoracis.origin: Sacrum, spinous processes of lumbar vertebrae, transverse processes of lower thoracic vertebrae
longissimus thoracis.insertion: Transverse processes of all thoracic vertebrae and lower 9-10 ribs
longissimus thoracis.action: Extension and lateral flexion of thoracic and lumbar spine
longissimus thoracis.innervation: Posterior rami of thoracic and lumbar spinal nerves

spinalis thoracis.innervation: Posterior rami of thoracic spinal nerves

semispinalis capitis.insertion: Between superior and inferior nuchal lines of occipital bone
semispinalis capitis.action: Extension and lateral flexion of head; contralateral rotation
semispinalis capitis.innervation: Posterior rami of cervical spinal nerves (C1-C6)

semispinalis cervicis.action: Extension and contralateral rotation of cervical spine
semispinalis cervicis.innervation: Posterior rami of cervical spinal nerves

semispinalis thoracis.action: Extension and contralateral rotation of thoracic spine
semispinalis thoracis.innervation: Posterior rami of thoracic spinal nerves

interspinalis.action: Extension of vertebral column; postural stabilization

intertransversarii.action: Lateral flexion of vertebral column; postural stabilization
intertransversarii.innervation: Anterior and posterior rami of spinal nerves

multifidus cervicis.insertion: Spinous processes of vertebrae 2-4 levels above
multifidus cervicis.action: Stabilizes cervical vertebrae; extension and contralateral rotation of cervical spine
multifidus cervicis.innervation: Posterior rami of cervical spinal nerves

multifidus thoracis.insertion: Spinous processes of vertebrae 2-4 levels above
multifidus thoracis.action: Stabilizes thoracic vertebrae; extension and contralateral rotation of thoracic spine
multifidus thoracis.innervation: Posterior rami of thoracic spinal nerves

multifidus lumborum.origin: Sacrum, posterior superior iliac spine, mamillary processes of lumbar vertebrae
multifidus lumborum.insertion: Spinous processes of vertebrae 2-4 levels above
multifidus lumborum.action: Key spinal stabilizer; extension and contralateral rotation of lumbar spine
multifidus lumborum.innervation: Posterior rami of lumbar spinal nerves

rotatores.insertion: Lamina and base of spinous process of vertebra above (brevis: 1 level; longus: 2 levels)
rotatores.action: Proprioception; assists extension and contralateral rotation of vertebral column

quadratus lumborum.insertion: Inferior border of 12th rib, transverse processes of L1-L4
quadratus lumborum.action: Lateral flexion of trunk; fixes 12th rib during deep inspiration; extension of lumbar spine

thoracolumbar fascia.origin: Spinous processes of thoracic and lumbar vertebrae, sacrum, iliac crest
thoracolumbar fascia.insertion: Transverse processes of lumbar vertebrae, 12th rib, internal oblique, transversus abdominis
thoracolumbar fascia.action: Provides attachment and load transfer between trunk muscles; stabilizes the lumbar spine
thoracolumbar fascia.notes: Three-layer fascial structure (posterior, middle, anterior) that encloses the deep back muscles

biceps brachii.origin: Short head: coracoid process. Long head: supraglenoid tubercle of scapula
biceps brachii.insertion: Radial tuberosity and bicipital aponeurosis into deep fascia of forearm
biceps brachii.action: Flexion of elbow, supination of forearm, assists shoulder flexion

triceps brachii.origin: Long head: infraglenoid tubercle. Lateral head: posterior humerus above radial groove. Medial head: posterior humerus below radial groove
triceps brachii.action: Extension of elbow; long head assists shoulder extension/adduction

brachialis.origin: Distal half of anterior surface of humerus
brachialis.insertion: Coronoid process and tuberosity of ulna

coracobrachialis.action: Flexion and adduction of arm at shoulder

anconeus.insertion: Lateral surface of olecranon and posterior proximal ulna
anconeus.action: Assists triceps in elbow extension; stabilizes elbow joint

brachioradialis.insertion: Lateral surface of distal radius (styloid process)
brachioradialis.action: Flexion of forearm; assists pronation and supination to neutral

pronator teres.origin: Humeral head: medial epicondyle. Ulnar head: coronoid process
pronator teres.insertion: Middle of lateral surface of radius

pronator quadratus.origin: Distal quarter of anterior surface of ulna
pronator quadratus.insertion: Distal quarter of anterior surface of radius

supinator.origin: Lateral epicondyle, radial collateral ligament, supinator crest of ulna
supinator.insertion: Lateral, posterior, and anterior surfaces of proximal radius

flexor carpi radialis.origin: Medial epicondyle of humerus (common flexor origin)
flexor carpi radialis.action: Flexion and abduction (radial deviation) of wrist

flexor carpi ulnaris.origin: Humeral head: medial epicondyle. Ulnar head: olecranon and posterior ulna
flexor carpi ulnaris.insertion: Pisiform, hook of hamate, base of 5th metacarpal
flexor carpi ulnaris.action: Flexion and adduction (ulnar deviation) of wrist

extensor carpi radialis brevis.origin: Lateral epicondyle of humerus (common extensor origin)

extensor carpi ulnaris.action: Extension and adduction (ulnar deviation) of wrist

flexor digitorum superficialis.origin: Medial epicondyle, coronoid process, anterior radius
flexor digitorum superficialis.action: Flexion of middle phalanges of digits 2-5; assists wrist flexion

flexor digitorum profundus.origin: Proximal three-quarters of medial and anterior ulna, interosseous membrane
flexor digitorum profundus.action: Flexion of distal phalanges of digits 2-5

extensor digitorum.action: Extension of digits 2-5 at metacarpophalangeal joints

extensor indicis.origin: Posterior surface of distal ulna and interosseous membrane
extensor indicis.action: Extension of index finger; assists wrist extension

extensor pollicis longus.origin: Posterior surface of middle third of ulna, interosseous membrane
extensor pollicis longus.insertion: Base of distal phalanx of thumb
extensor pollicis longus.action: Extension of thumb at IP joint; assists wrist extension

extensor pollicis brevis.origin: Posterior surface of distal radius and interosseous membrane
extensor pollicis brevis.insertion: Base of proximal phalanx of thumb
extensor pollicis brevis.action: Extension of thumb at MCP joint

abductor pollicis longus.origin: Posterior surfaces of radius, ulna, and interosseous membrane
abductor pollicis longus.action: Abduction and extension of thumb at CMC joint

palmaris longus.origin: Medial epicondyle of humerus (common flexor origin)
palmaris longus.action: Flexion of wrist; tenses palmar aponeurosis

flexor pollicis longus.origin: Anterior surface of radius and adjacent interosseous membrane
flexor pollicis longus.insertion: Base of distal phalanx of thumb
flexor pollicis longus.action: Flexion of thumb (IP and MCP joints)

external oblique.insertion: Linea alba, pubic tubercle, anterior iliac crest
external oblique.action: Bilateral: flexion of trunk, compression of abdomen. Unilateral: contralateral rotation, ipsilateral lateral flexion
external oblique.innervation: Intercostal nerves T7-T12, iliohypogastric and ilioinguinal nerves

internal oblique.origin: Thoracolumbar fascia, iliac crest, lateral inguinal ligament
internal oblique.insertion: Inferior borders of ribs 10-12, linea alba, pubic crest
internal oblique.action: Bilateral: flexion of trunk, compression of abdomen. Unilateral: ipsilateral rotation and lateral flexion
internal oblique.innervation: Intercostal nerves T7-T12, iliohypogastric and ilioinguinal nerves (L1)

rectus abdominis.insertion: Xiphoid process and costal cartilages of ribs 5-7
rectus abdominis.action: Flexion of trunk; compression of abdomen; stabilization of pelvis during walking

transversus abdominis.origin: Internal surfaces of costal cartilages 7-12, thoracolumbar fascia, iliac crest, lateral inguinal ligament
transversus abdominis.insertion: Linea alba, pubic crest, pectineal line
transversus abdominis.action: Compression of abdomen; core stabilization; increases intra-abdominal pressure
transversus abdominis.innervation: Intercostal nerves T7-T12, iliohypogastric and ilioinguinal nerves (L1)

diaphragm.origin: Xiphoid process, costal cartilages 7-12, L1-L3 vertebral bodies, medial/lateral arcuate ligaments
diaphragm.action: Primary muscle of respiration; contraction increases thoracic volume

transversus thoracis.origin: Posterior surface of lower sternum and xiphoid process
transversus thoracis.action: Depresses costal cartilages; assists forced expiration

gluteus maximus.origin: Posterior ilium, sacrum, coccyx, thoracolumbar fascia, sacrotuberous ligament
gluteus maximus.insertion: Iliotibial tract and gluteal tuberosity of femur
gluteus maximus.action: Extension and lateral rotation of hip; upper fibers: abduction; lower fibers: adduction

gluteus medius.origin: External surface of ilium between anterior and posterior gluteal lines
gluteus medius.insertion: Lateral surface of greater trochanter of femur
gluteus medius.action: Abduction and medial rotation of hip; stabilizes pelvis during gait

gluteus minimus.origin: External surface of ilium between anterior and inferior gluteal lines
gluteus minimus.action: Abduction and medial rotation of hip

piriformis.action: Lateral rotation, abduction of flexed hip; stabilizes hip joint

obturator internus.origin: Internal surface of obturator membrane and surrounding bone
obturator internus.action: Lateral rotation of hip; abduction of flexed hip
obturator internus.innervation: Nerve to obturator internus (L5, S1, S2)

obturator externus.origin: External surface of obturator membrane and surrounding bone
obturator externus.innervation: Posterior branch of obturator nerve (L3, L4)

iliacus.insertion: Lesser trochanter of femur (with psoas major)

psoas major.origin: Transverse processes and vertebral bodies of T12-L5
psoas major.action: Flexion of hip; lateral flexion of trunk

tensor fasciae latae.origin: Anterior superior iliac spine (ASIS) and anterior iliac crest
tensor fasciae latae.insertion: Iliotibial tract (which attaches to lateral tibial condyle)
tensor fasciae latae.action: Flexion, abduction, and medial rotation of hip; stabilizes knee in extension

gemellus superior.insertion: Medial surface of greater trochanter (with obturator internus tendon)
gemellus superior.action: Lateral rotation of hip; abduction of flexed hip
gemellus superior.innervation: Nerve to obturator internus (L5, S1, S2)

gemellus inferior.insertion: Medial surface of greater trochanter (with obturator internus tendon)
gemellus inferior.action: Lateral rotation of hip; abduction of flexed hip
gemellus inferior.innervation: Nerve to quadratus femoris (L5, S1, S2)

pectineus.origin: Pectineal line of pubis (between pubic tubercle and iliopubic eminence)
pectineus.insertion: Pectineal line of femur (between lesser trochanter and linea aspera)
pectineus.action: Adduction and flexion of hip; assists medial rotation
pectineus.innervation: Femoral nerve (L2, L3), sometimes obturator nerve

coccygeus.action: Supports pelvic floor; flexes coccyx; assists in pelvic organ support

iliococcygeus.origin: Tendinous arch of levator ani (obturator fascia) and ischial spine
iliococcygeus.action: Supports pelvic viscera; elevates pelvic floor
iliococcygeus.innervation: Branches of S3-S4 via nerve to levator ani

pubococcygeus.origin: Posterior surface of pubic body and anterior tendinous arch
pubococcygeus.action: Supports pelvic viscera; constricts lower rectum and vagina
pubococcygeus.innervation: Branches of S3-S4 via nerve to levator ani

puborectalis.insertion: Forms a sling around the anorectal junction
puborectalis.action: Maintains anorectal angle; essential for fecal continence
puborectalis.innervation: Branches of S3-S4 via nerve to levator ani

external anal sphincter.action: Closes anal canal; maintains fecal continence
external anal sphincter.innervation: Inferior rectal nerve (S2-S4) via pudendal nerve

rectus femoris.origin: Anterior inferior iliac spine (straight head); superior acetabulum (reflected head)
rectus femoris.action: Extension of knee; flexion of hip

vastus lateralis.origin: Greater trochanter, intertrochanteric line, linea aspera

vastus medialis.origin: Intertrochanteric line, linea aspera, medial supracondylar line
vastus medialis.insertion: Tibial tuberosity via patellar ligament; medial border of patella
vastus medialis.action: Extension of knee; stabilizes patella (especially VMO portion)

vastus intermedius.origin: Anterior and lateral surfaces of femoral shaft

biceps femoris.origin: Long head: ischial tuberosity. Short head: linea aspera, lateral supracondylar line
biceps femoris.insertion: Head of fibula; lateral condyle of tibia
biceps femoris.action: Flexion of knee; extension and lateral rotation of hip (long head)
biceps femoris.innervation: Long head: tibial division. Short head: common fibular division of sciatic nerve

semitendinosus.insertion: Medial surface of proximal tibia (pes anserinus)
semitendinosus.action: Flexion of knee; extension and medial rotation of hip
semitendinosus.innervation: Tibial division of sciatic nerve (L5, S1, S2)

semimembranosus.action: Flexion of knee; extension and medial rotation of hip
semimembranosus.innervation: Tibial division of sciatic nerve (L5, S1, S2)

sartorius.insertion: Medial surface of proximal tibia (pes anserinus)
sartorius.action: Flexion, abduction, and lateral rotation of hip; flexion of knee

gracilis.origin: Body and inferior ramus of pubis
gracilis.insertion: Medial surface of proximal tibia (pes anserinus)
gracilis.action: Adduction of hip; flexion and medial rotation of knee

adductor longus.action: Adduction of hip; assists flexion and medial rotation

adductor brevis.origin: Body and inferior ramus of pubis
adductor brevis.insertion: Pectineal line and proximal linea aspera

adductor magnus.origin: Inferior pubic ramus, ischial ramus, ischial tuberosity
adductor magnus.insertion: Linea aspera, medial supracondylar line, adductor tubercle
adductor magnus.action: Adduction of hip; adductor part: flexion; hamstring part: extension
adductor magnus.innervation: Obturator nerve (adductor part), tibial part of sciatic nerve (hamstring part)

iliotibial tract.origin: Tensor fasciae latae and gluteus maximus (iliac crest, anterior superior iliac spine)
iliotibial tract.insertion: Gerdy's tubercle on the lateral condyle of tibia
iliotibial tract.action: Stabilizes the knee in extension; assists abduction of hip; lateral stabilizer during gait
iliotibial tract.notes: Thick band of deep fascia running along lateral thigh; common source of IT band syndrome in runners

gastrocnemius.origin: Medial head: medial condyle of femur. Lateral head: lateral condyle of femur
gastrocnemius.action: Plantarflexion of ankle; assists flexion of knee

soleus.origin: Posterior surface of head and upper quarter of fibula; soleal line and middle third of tibia

tibialis anterior.origin: Lateral condyle and upper two-thirds of lateral surface of tibia
tibialis anterior.insertion: Medial cuneiform and base of 1st metatarsal

tibialis posterior.origin: Posterior surfaces of tibia, fibula, and interosseous membrane
tibialis posterior.insertion: Navicular, cuneiforms, cuboid, bases of metatarsals 2-4
tibialis posterior.action: Plantarflexion and inversion of foot; supports medial arch

fibularis longus.origin: Head and upper two-thirds of lateral surface of fibula
fibularis longus.insertion: Base of 1st metatarsal and medial cuneiform
fibularis longus.action: Eversion and plantarflexion of foot; supports transverse arch

fibularis brevis.origin: Lower two-thirds of lateral surface of fibula

fibularis tertius.origin: Distal third of anterior fibula and interosseous membrane

plantaris.origin: Lateral supracondylar ridge of femur, oblique popliteal ligament
plantaris.action: Weak plantarflexion of ankle; weak flexion of knee

popliteus.origin: Lateral condyle of femur, lateral meniscus
popliteus.insertion: Posterior surface of proximal tibia (above soleal line)
popliteus.action: "Unlocks" knee by medially rotating tibia; flexion of knee

extensor digitorum longus.origin: Lateral condyle of tibia, upper three-quarters of anterior fibula
extensor digitorum longus.insertion: Middle and distal phalanges of digits 2-5
extensor digitorum longus.action: Extension of digits 2-5; dorsiflexion and eversion of foot

extensor hallucis longus.origin: Middle part of anterior fibula and interosseous membrane
extensor hallucis longus.insertion: Dorsal surface of distal phalanx of great toe
extensor hallucis longus.action: Extension of great toe; dorsiflexion of ankle

extensor hallucis brevis.insertion: Base of proximal phalanx of great toe
extensor hallucis brevis.action: Extension of great toe at MTP joint

flexor digitorum longus.origin: Posterior surface of middle third of tibia
flexor digitorum longus.action: Flexion of digits 2-5; plantarflexion of ankle

flexor hallucis longus.insertion: Base of distal phalanx of great toe
flexor hallucis longus.action: Flexion of great toe; plantarflexion of ankle; supports medial arch

calcaneal tendon.action: Transmits force for plantarflexion of ankle
calcaneal tendon.notes: Strongest and thickest tendon in the body; approximately 15cm long

abductor hallucis.origin: Medial process of calcaneal tuberosity, flexor retinaculum, plantar aponeurosis
abductor hallucis.insertion: Medial side of base of proximal phalanx of great toe
abductor hallucis.action: Abduction and flexion of great toe

flexor digitorum brevis.origin: Medial process of calcaneal tuberosity, plantar aponeurosis
flexor digitorum brevis.action: Flexion of middle phalanges of digits 2-5

flexor accessorius.origin: Medial and lateral surfaces of calcaneus
flexor accessorius.action: Assists flexor digitorum longus by correcting oblique pull

flexor hallucis brevis.origin: Plantar surface of cuboid and lateral cuneiforms
flexor hallucis brevis.insertion: Both sides of base of proximal phalanx of great toe
flexor hallucis brevis.action: Flexion of great toe at MTP joint

abductor digiti minimi.origin: Lateral process of calcaneal tuberosity, plantar aponeurosis
abductor digiti minimi.insertion: Lateral side of base of proximal phalanx of 5th toe

flexor digiti minimi brevis.origin: Base of 5th metatarsal, sheath of fibularis longus
flexor digiti minimi brevis.insertion: Lateral side of base of proximal phalanx of 5th toe
flexor digiti minimi brevis.action: Flexion of 5th toe at MTP joint

abductor pollicis brevis.insertion: Lateral side of base of proximal phalanx of thumb
abductor pollicis brevis.action: Abduction of thumb at CMC joint

opponens pollicis.action: Opposition of thumb (medial rotation and flexion at CMC joint)

flexor pollicis brevis.origin: Superficial head: flexor retinaculum, trapezium. Deep head: trapezoid, capitate
flexor pollicis brevis.insertion: Base of proximal phalanx of thumb
flexor pollicis brevis.action: Flexion of thumb at MCP joint
flexor pollicis brevis.innervation: Superficial head: median nerve. Deep head: ulnar nerve (C8, T1)

adductor pollicis.origin: Oblique head: capitate, bases of 2nd-3rd metacarpals. Transverse head: shaft of 3rd metacarpal
adductor pollicis.insertion: Medial side of base of proximal phalanx of thumb
adductor pollicis.innervation: Deep branch of ulnar nerve (C8, T1)
